morning_resolutions.resolution: Done at 11:48 BST — product sign-off completed and ticket closed.

morning_resolutions.summary: New Global Strategy Hub / Sub Hub Refinements
morning_resolutions.resolution: Done at 09:26 BST — released, no further action needed.
Done at 11:54 BST — cleared QA and released. Gabriel had flagged this as a priority to release today; delivered.
Done at 12:39 BST — Gabriel flagged this as a release priority this morning; now complete.

action_items.action: URGENT: Respond to Chloe Christie in #gdc-product-design-pod (12:42 BST) — she tagged @Mike Pett @Miguel Migneco asking which logo type to use for the NZ Instagram Headers Widget full implementation (GDCU-8840). Two logo options: (A) 'Operators - 150px - Brand Product - 474 - Transparent' for rectangles, or (B) 'Operators - 300px - Brand Product - 474 - Square' for circles. Chloe says not every brand will have a background colour for the circles. She's asked Miguel to review Origins and confirm availability. Chloe also offered a call. This is the same ticket where you still owe Chloe the EVE logo information from 16 Apr — both are outstanding.

Escalate Operator List Redesign block (GDCU-8619) — still Blocked, 8 days since 15 Apr, no change since morning. If Michael Butlin was not resolved in the 10:00 Product Sync, raise directly with Colin Brannigan in the 15:00 1:1. Ciara Carroll has 3+ design iterations waiting and the wider oplist redesign programme (including GDCU-8801) is dependent on this decision.

NEW — Product sign-off on Ace AI Full Football Coverage (GDCU-8714). QA completed by Shane McGrath at 11:04 BST: all 8 leagues confirmed working including Belgium Pro League (previously outstanding). All search patterns set up. Deployed to qa16.gambling.com/ace. Mike is a named stakeholder. Ready for product sign-off and production release.

NEW — Respond to Chloe Christie's comment on Always On Betting Tips Page (GDCU-8512) at 11:59 BST. Mike left 'Signed off from product' at 10:09, but Chloe subsequently tagged @Mike Pett @Miguel Migneco requesting a revised dev-ready design: (1) only elements ready for implementation, (2) use existing site components (e.g. current offer cards, not proposed new ones). Two design versions exist (single full-width banner vs multi-banner widget). Confirm revised brief with Miguel.

Confirm design direction on CA/FR Casino Oplist Redesign (GDCU-8801) — carry-forward from morning, no change. Diana Thurmann has approved initial mockups; Josh Smith has asked Ciara to move to dev-ready. Mike's sign-off on direction unblocks Ciara from progressing.

Prep for GDC Data Gaps meeting at 16:30 BST (Chris Raulli, Colin Brannigan, Vic Dadson, David Muleropino). Use the 16:00 Mike/Vic Sync to align with Vic on skill gap findings and agree the narrative. Concrete examples to bring: GDCU-8869 (PSI/performance capability), GDCU-8810 (Apple/Google Dev Accounts, still Blocked), GDCU-8893 (Design System gap — Ciara actively working with limited resourcing).

AWARE: GDCU-8546 (Tinder Style Offer Match) moved BACK to In Progress at 11:47 BST — was In Product this morning awaiting sign-off, now returned to Jessica Dordevic for more work. Not currently actionable. Track for when it next reaches In Product.

AWARE: Ciara Carroll and Miguel Migneco actively discussing Design System component naming and white-container style decisions in #gdc-product-led-growth (10:31–11:46 BST). Two options: (1) container + outline stroke + drop shadow (like op cards), or (2) no container/no stroke + drop shadow (like news cards/IUJ). No direct Mike input requested yet, but the decision will shape GDCU-8893 scope.

AWARE: Desktop PSI geo-redirect race condition (GDCU-8869) — Sanja Budic also picked up GDCU-8863 (Incorrect Ktag CT Value on Gambling.com Operator Pages) today at 11:39. Both active. No Mike action needed.

needs_input.context: BLOCKED since 15 Apr (8 days) — no change since morning. Awaiting feedback from Michael Butlin. Chloe confirmed the block. Ciara has delivered 3+ design iterations. If not resolved in the 10:00 Product Sync, escalate to Colin Brannigan in the 15:00 1:1. Blocking the wider oplist redesign programme.

NEW since morning. QA completed by Shane McGrath at 11:04 BST. All 8+ leagues (England L1/L2, Spain, Germany, Italy, Belgium, Peru, India, Australia) verified on qa16.gambling.com/ace. Mike is a named stakeholder. Straightforward sign-off needed to release.

NEW since morning. Mike signed off at 10:09 but Chloe Christie has since tagged Mike and Miguel requesting a revised dev-ready design using only currently-implementable elements and existing site components. Two Figma versions exist. Mike needs to confirm the revised brief and ensure Miguel acts on Chloe's feedback.
